tv.title: Fullmetal Alchemist: Brotherhood
tv.blurb: Widely considered the gold standard of anime. Perfect pacing, zero filler, and incredible philosophical themes around sacrifice and ambition.

tv.title: Attack on Titan
tv.blurb: A masterclass in plot twists, political intrigue, and high-stakes tension. The storytelling is tightly wound from the very first episode.

tv.title: Hunter x Hunter (2011)
tv.blurb: Don't let the bright art style fool you — this series evolves into one of the darkest, most psychologically complex power systems in anime.

tv.title: Death Note
tv.blurb: The ultimate high-IQ cat-and-mouse game between a brilliant high schooler with a death god's notebook and an eccentric detective.

tv.title: Cowboy Bebop
tv.blurb: Pure style, a legendary jazz soundtrack, and an episodic exploration of loneliness, regret, and running from your past.

tv.title: Vinland Saga
tv.blurb: A breathtaking Viking epic that transitions from a brutal revenge story into a deep, emotional masterpiece about peace and redemption.

tv.title: Steins;Gate
tv.blurb: One of the best-written time-travel thrillers in fiction. It starts slow as a quirky slice-of-life before pulling the rug out from under you.

tv.title: Monster
tv.blurb: A slow-burn masterpiece following a brain surgeon who accidentally saves the life of a young boy who grows up to be a charismatic serial killer.

tv.title: Code Geass: Lelouch of the Rebellion
tv.blurb: Features one of the greatest anti-heroes in fiction. It's essentially chess on a global scale with mechs and mind control.

tv.title: Jujutsu Kaisen
tv.blurb: Modern shonen at its peak, offering some of the most fluid, jaw-droppingly animated combat sequences of the last decade.

films.title: Spirited Away
films.blurb: Studio Ghibli's crowning achievement. A magical, beautifully animated masterpiece that won an Academy Award for a reason.

films.title: Grave of the Fireflies
films.blurb: A deeply emotional, devastatingly realistic look at two siblings trying to survive in Japan during the final months of WWII.

films.title: Your Name
films.blurb: A visual marvel by Makoto Shinkai. It seamlessly blends a body-swapping comedy with a massive, high-stakes cosmic mystery.

films.title: Princess Mononoke
films.blurb: A gritty, beautiful clash between industrializing humanity and the ancient gods of nature. There are no clear villains, just complex perspectives.

films.title: Howl's Moving Castle
films.blurb: Whimsical, visual storytelling at its best, focusing on a young woman cursed with old age and a vain wizard living in a walking fortress.

films.title: A Silent Voice
films.blurb: A profoundly moving story dealing with heavy themes of bullying, disability (deafness), mental health, and ultimate self-forgiveness.

films.title: Akira
films.blurb: The film that broke anime into the global mainstream. Visually unmatched cel animation featuring a gritty, neon-drenched dystopian Neo-Tokyo.

films.title: Ghost in the Shell
films.blurb: A deep philosophical dive into what constitutes a human soul in a completely cybernetic world. This heavily inspired The Matrix.

films.title: Perfect Blue
films.blurb: A deeply unsettling, brilliant look at the dark side of pop-idol stardom, blurring the lines between reality, dreams, and online delusion.
